text.title: Python Type Hints Adoption Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure for incremental typing adoption: from an untyped module to a CI-gated typed file.

step.name: detect-state
step.action: Detect python_version from `pyproject.toml [project] requires-python`. Detect existing typing coverage with `mypy --html-report` or `ruff check --statistics --select ANN`. Classify repo as greenfield (≥80% typed), legacy (<50% typed), or library (PyPI-published).

step.name: install-config
step.action: Copy `templates/mypy.toml` into `pyproject.toml`. Adjust `python_version` to detected value. For mode=legacy, replace `strict = true` with `files = ["src/api/handlers.py", ...]` allowlist seeded with the public-API manifest. For mode=greenfield, keep `strict = true`. For mode=library, add `disallow_any_generics = true`.
step.decision-gate: If repo mixes Pydantic v1 and Pydantic v2 — abort and remediate v1 first; this methodology assumes v2.

step.name: wire-ci
step.action: Add `templates/typecheck-touched.sh` to `.github/workflows/typecheck.yml`. Set `BASE_REF` to `${{ github.base_ref }}`. Fail the job on mypy non-zero exit.

step.name: annotate-touched-file
step.input: one Python file the current task is editing
step.action: Annotate every public signature (args + return). Annotate module-level vars where mypy errors. Replace `Optional[X]` with `X | None` and `List[T]` with `list[T]` via `ruff check --fix --select UP007 <file>`. Run `mypy --strict <file>`. Fix one error class per pass: no-untyped-def → var-annotated → arg-type → return-value.
step.decision-gate: If a single file hits >30 errors and contains heavy dynamic metaprogramming → mark it `# mypy: ignore-errors` at top with a justifying comment and escalate to a refactor task; do not deep-type metaprogramming code.

step.name: extend-allowlist
step.action: Append the file path to `[tool.mypy] files = [...]` (legacy mode) or rely on global strict (greenfield mode). Commit. Re-run CI to confirm. Repeat from step 4 on the next file.
step.output: growing strict-typed surface; CI gate never regresses
